vulnerabilities.description: uint16_t alloc_size = length * 2 overflows when length >= 32768, wrapping to a small value and causing malloc to allocate far less memory than the caller expects.
memcpy writes length*2 bytes into field->value; when alloc_field_value's integer overflow produced a smaller-than-expected buffer, this writes far past the allocation boundary.
out->length is read from the packet without verifying that buf_len >= 4 + out->length; the subsequent fill_field call reads past the end of the supplied buffer.
alloc_field_value can return NULL on malloc failure; the return value is stored in out->value and immediately passed to fill_field without a NULL check, causing a NULL pointer dereference.
pkt->fields[i].value is attacker-controlled data from the network packet and is passed directly as the format string to snprintf, enabling format string injection.
offset is incremented without being checked against out_len; repeated field summaries can push offset past the end of out, causing an out-of-bounds write.
malloc(sizeof(Packet)) can return NULL; pkt is dereferenced on the very next line without any NULL check, causing a NULL pointer dereference crash.
When parse_tlv returns -1 mid-loop, the field->value buffers already allocated for fields 0..i-1 are leaked; only pkt itself is freed before returning NULL.

cross_function.description: alloc_field_value produces an undersized buffer due to integer overflow; parse_tlv passes the result directly to fill_field, which writes length*2 bytes and overflows the too-small allocation.

alloc_field_value allocates field->value inside parse_tlv for each TLV field; when parse_tlv later returns -1, process_packet frees only pkt and returns NULL, leaking all previously allocated field->value buffers.
